narrative:
The stress score sits at 41 out of 100, placing the market in cautious territory within a business-as-usual quadrant. Credit markets offer little alarm — high-yield spreads at 259 basis points and investment-grade at 72 basis points both carry negative z-scores, indicating spreads remain compressed relative to recent history — but the equity picture has grown more mixed, with five of eleven sectors in the red over five days and today's session seeing communication services and consumer discretionary lead the downside.

The standout move today is the continued weakness in communication services, with XLC down 2.1% on the day after already losing 4.0% over the past five sessions, suggesting the prior week's rotation has now extended into a more sustained sector-level repositioning rather than noise. A decisive break of the VIX above 20, or a widening in high-yield spreads beyond their recent range toward a positive z-score, would signal that the cautious reading is hardening into something that warrants a reassessment of the current business-as-usual regime.
